steps.title: Use hemisphere volume
steps.content:
A hemisphere is half of a sphere. Since a sphere has volume $\frac{4}{3}\pi r^3$, a hemisphere has volume
$$V=\frac{1}{2}\cdot\frac{4}{3}\pi r^3=\frac{2}{3}\pi r^3$$

steps.title: Substitute the radius
steps.content:
Use the given radius, $r=66$, and compute the cubed value before multiplying by $\pi$.
$$V=\frac{2}{3}\pi(66)^3$$
$$66^3=287{,}496$$
$$V=191{,}664\pi$$

steps.title: Approximate the volume
steps.content:
Use $\pi$ as $3.14159$ to estimate the volume and choose the closest listed value.
$$191{,}664(3.14159)=602{,}138.51376$$
The closest value is $602{,}000$. The answer is D.
